summary: Scorching summer show with fiery Half-Step opener and heavy jams
blurb: The set bursts open with a killer Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo, followed by a soaring Franklin’s Tower despite a few lyrical slips. Highlights include the intense Estimated Prophet, seamless Black Peter → Good Lovin' transition and an inspired Lost Sailor → Saint of Circumstance jam. Overall the band is locked in, delivering a high‑energy performance.
review: On July 25, 1982 at Tempe’s Compton Terrace Amphitheatre the Dead turned up the heat for what many fans now cite as one of the summer tour’s top outings. Jerry launched the night with an exuberant Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo that set a joyous tone, and even though he stumbled through three verses of Franklin’s Tower his guitar remained on fire throughout the first set. Brent’s keyboards shone especially during a weighty Estimated Prophet, while Bob’s spontaneous lyric about freedom added emotional depth to Lost Sailor → Saint of Circumstance. The second set kept the momentum rolling: Scarlet Begonias into Fire On The Mountain flowed naturally, Samson & Delilah rocked hard, and Crazy Fingers sparked a tight groove that segued into an expansive Estimated Prophet. A highlight for many is the flawless Black Peter → Good Lovin' transition that capped the night before a heartfelt encore of Don’t Ease Me In. Across multiple audience and soundboard sources the performance feels “sick‑o” – high‑energy, tightly locked in, and captured with crystal‑clear clarity on the recommended audience tape.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All four recordings are marked positive; reviewers consistently praise the set’s energy, highlight multiple songs (Franklin's Tower, Half-Step, Estimated Prophet, Lost Sailor → Saint), and note no significant negative comments about performance quality.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear audience tape that captures the full show with immersive sound and no dropouts, while highlighting the night’s standout jams.

key_highlights: Franklin's Tower shines despite a few lyrical slips, showcasing Jerry’s guitar fire.
Estimated Prophet delivers a heavy, keyboard‑driven jam highlighted by Brent’s playing.
Half-Step opener sets an exuberant tone and is praised as a killer start.
Seamless Black Peter → Good Lovin' transition adds a groovy close to the second set.

band_performance.Jerry: Strong guitar throughout with a fiery Half‑Step opener; fumbles three verses on Franklin’s Tower but overall delivers solid lead work.
band_performance.Bob: Emotional vocal delivery and spontaneous lyric about freedom during Lost Sailor add depth to the jam.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard contributions are amazing, especially driving the heavy Estimated Prophet version.
